content_markdown: A $60\text{ kg}$ person riding a bicycle puts all of her weight on each pedal in turn when climbing a hill. The pedals rotate in a circle of radius $20\text{ cm}$. Estimate the maximum torque that is exerted by the person.

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a bicycle chain wheel with a pedal arm at an angle. The radius of the pedal rotation is labeled as 20 cm.

vibe_explanation: Maximum torque occurs when the force is applied perpendicular to the lever arm. The force is the person's weight $W = mg \approx 60 \times 10 = 600\text{ N}$. The lever arm (radius) is $0.20\text{ m}$. Thus, $\tau = F \times r = 600 \times 0.20 = 120\text{ Nm}$.
